title: Clinician Override Taxonomy for AI-Assisted Clinical Decision Making
description: Classification of clinician-AI interaction levels based on degree of AI autonomy and human oversight
source: Adapted from clinical decision support literature and regulatory frameworks

types.name: AI Output Not Shown
types.description: Clinician makes decision without AI. Used as baseline control arm.
types.use_case: Control arm in human-AI collaboration studies

types.name: AI Output Shown, No Action Required
types.description: AI output is displayed but clinician is not required to acknowledge or act on it.
types.regulatory_class: Clinical Decision Support (may be exempt from SaMD)

types.name: AI Output Shown, Clinician Must Acknowledge
types.description: AI output is displayed and clinician must acknowledge they have seen it before proceeding.

types.name: AI Output Shown, Override Requires Justification
types.description: AI recommendation is displayed and clinician must document a reason if they disagree.
types.use_case: Triage recommendations, diagnostic suggestions with audit trail

types.name: AI Recommendation with Mandatory Protocol
types.description: AI recommendation triggers a protocol that must be followed unless formally overridden with documentation.
types.use_case: Critical finding alerts, sepsis protocols, stroke pathways

types.name: AI Acts Autonomously with Human Notification
types.description: AI takes autonomous action (e.g., orders scan, sends alert) and notifies clinician afterward.
types.use_case: Autonomous diagnostic AI (e.g., IDx-DR), automated triage routing
